topic: Identifying and preventing common errors in for loops, specifically infinite loops that crash browsers and off-by-one iteration errors.

concepts.name: convergence
concepts.explanation: The idea that your loop variable must move from its starting value toward the exit condition so they eventually 'meet'.
concepts.why_it_matters: If the variable moves away from the condition or stays the same, the loop will never end.

concepts.name: infinite loop
concepts.explanation: A loop where the condition remains true forever because the variable never reaches the exit threshold.
concepts.why_it_matters: It consumes all available memory (stack overflow) and causes the browser to freeze or crash.

concepts.name: off-by-one error
concepts.explanation: A mistake where a loop runs one time too many or one time too few, often caused by confusion between starting at 0 and using `<` vs `<=`.
concepts.why_it_matters: It leads to incorrect counts or unexpected behavior in patterns and data processing.

code_examples.demonstrates: An infinite loop caused by decrementing a variable that is already below the exit condition.
code_examples.common_issue: The browser will freeze and become unresponsive because x will always be less than 10.

code_examples.demonstrates: An infinite loop caused by a variable mismatch (updating 'y' instead of the loop control variable 'x').
code_examples.common_issue: The loop condition (x < 10) never changes because x is never updated.

code_examples.demonstrates: A standard loop starting at 0 and running exactly 10 times.
code_examples.common_issue: Students often miscalculate the total iterations when starting at 0.

code_examples.demonstrates: A loop that runs 11 times because it includes the value 10.
code_examples.common_issue: Using `<=` when you only wanted to reach the value, not include it.

mistakes_and_fixes.symptom: The browser freezes and becomes unresponsive/crashes.
mistakes_and_fixes.cause: Infinite loop: the variable is moving away from the exit condition (e.g., decrementing when it should increment).
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Ensure the variable 'converges' with the condition.

mistakes_and_fixes.cause: Variable mismatch: updating the wrong variable in the increment/decrement step.
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Check that the variable being updated is the same one used in the condition.

mistakes_and_fixes.symptom: The loop runs 11 times instead of 10 (or vice versa).
mistakes_and_fixes.cause: Off-by-one error: confusion between starting at 0 and using `<` vs `<=`.
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Carefully count the iterations: if starting at 0 and using `<`, it runs exactly 'n' times.

key_moments.description: Instructor warns that they are about to break the browser, emphasizing the danger of loops.
The browser actually becomes unresponsive, demonstrating the real-world consequence of an infinite loop.
Summary of preventative measures: save work, check convergence, and turn off autoplay.

student_questions: Why did my browser just freeze?
My code isn't doing anything and I can't click anything in the browser.
How do I stop a loop that won't end?
Why is my loop running 11 times when I only wanted it to run 10?
How do I make sure my loop actually stops?
Why is it important to save my work before writing a loop?
What happens if I use x-- instead of x++ in a loop starting at 0?
Does starting at 0 change how many times a loop runs?
